extraction.one_line_summary: DUMOULIN Tom won 2017 Giro d'Italia by 31 seconds over NAIRO Quintana, defending 53-second lead in Stage 21 ITT despite losing 22 seconds.
tactical_patterns: DUMOULIN paced conservatively through intermediate checkpoint (19 seconds down on VAN EMDEN) while protecting overall lead rather than stage hunting.
PINOT cracked unexpectedly in final ITT losing 33+ seconds to DUMOULIN, revealing weaker time trial form than pre-race handicapping suggested.
ZAKARIN took white jersey on final stage, indicating young rider classification was still contested despite DUMOULIN's overall focus.

rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: positive: defended 53-second GC lead by losing only 22 seconds to Quintana, showing elite TT pacing discipline under extreme pressure
rider_signals.NAIRO Quintana: neutral: closed 22 seconds on Dumoulin in final ITT but insufficient from 53-second deficit, confirming time trial deficit
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: negative: lost 33 seconds to Dumoulin when needing to defend 43-second GC gap, indicating poor final ITT form
rider_signals.VAN EMDEN Jos: positive: first Grand Tour stage win with fastest time, showing late-race TT peak form
rider_signals.ZAKARIN Ilnur: positive: seized white jersey on final stage suggesting persistent GC aggression through final days
